questions.questionText: Choose the word closest in meaning to CANDID.
questions.explanation: Candid means truthful and straightforward in speech or expression, which is exactly what frank means. The other options describe secrecy, intelligence, or carefulness, none of which match candid.

questions.questionText: Select the word most similar in meaning to EPHEMERAL.
questions.explanation: Ephemeral describes something that lasts for a very short time, and transient carries the same sense of brief duration. Eternal is its opposite, while robust and significant are unrelated in meaning.

questions.questionText: Pick the closest synonym of OBSTREPEROUS.
questions.explanation: Obstreperous means noisy, defiant, and difficult to control, so unruly is the best match. Obedient is an antonym, while transparent and melancholic refer to clarity and sadness respectively.

questions.questionText: Choose the word most nearly OPPOSITE in meaning to SCARCE.
questions.explanation: Scarce means available in very small quantities, so its opposite is abundant, meaning existing in large amounts. Rare is a synonym of scarce, not an antonym.

questions.questionText: Select the word most nearly OPPOSITE in meaning to FRUGAL.
questions.explanation: Frugal means careful and sparing with money, so its opposite is extravagant, meaning spending freely and wastefully. Economical, stingy, and prudent are all close in spirit to frugal.

questions.questionText: Pick the word most nearly OPPOSITE in meaning to OBDURATE.
questions.explanation: Obdurate describes a person who stubbornly refuses to change their opinion or soften their attitude. Yielding, meaning flexible and willing to give way, is therefore the correct antonym; stubborn and hardened are synonyms of obdurate.

questions.questionText: What is the meaning of the idiom 'to let the cat out of the bag'?
questions.explanation: The idiom 'to let the cat out of the bag' means to disclose a secret, usually without intending to. It is often used when confidential plans or surprises are revealed too early.

questions.questionText: What is the meaning of the idiom 'to throw down the gauntlet'?
questions.explanation: Throwing down the gauntlet comes from the medieval custom of a knight throwing his glove to the ground to challenge an opponent to combat. Today it means to openly challenge or confront someone.

questions.questionText: Choose the one word that means 'a person who can speak many languages'.
questions.explanation: A polyglot is specifically a person who knows and is able to use several languages. A linguist studies the science of language, an orator is a skilled public speaker, and an interpreter translates speech between languages.

questions.questionText: Choose the one word that means 'one who believes that nothing can be known about the existence of God'.
questions.explanation: An agnostic holds that the existence of God is unknown or unknowable, which is different from an atheist, who denies that God exists. A heretic opposes accepted religious doctrine, and a fanatic holds extreme, uncritical zeal.
